title: Clinical AI Attribution Schema (CAAS) v0.1.1
description: Vendor-agnostic, jurisdiction-configurable schema for clinical AI attribution events.
, Copyright 2026 Sarah Curtright. CAVAL™ and CAVAL Layer™ are trademarks of Sarah Curtright. Licensed under CC BY 4.0: https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode

caae_version.description: Schema version. Must be '0.1.1' for this release.

event.description: Identifies the attribution event itself. All fields required.

event_id.description: Globally unique UUID v4 identifier for this event.

event_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 datetime with timezone — when the event was generated.

caae_version.description: Schema version at time of event generation.

parent_event_id.description: UUID of prior event if this is a supplement or correction. Null for original events.

emitting_system.description: Identifier of the system that generated this event.

decision_context.description: Describes the clinical decision point at which AI was active.

decision_type.description: Type of clinical decision at this point.

decision_type_free_text.description: Required when decision_type is 'other'. Documents the actual decision type.

decision_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 datetime with timezone — when the clinical decision occurred.

patient_id_hash.description: SHA-256 salted hash of patient identifier. Never store raw patient identifiers.

clinician_id_hash.description: SHA-256 salted hash of clinician identifier. Never store raw clinician identifiers.

clinician_role.description: Role of the clinician making the decision.

facility_id.description: Identifier of the health system or facility.

fhir_resource_reference.description: FHIR R4 resource type and ID of the decision object e.g. 'MedicationRequest/mr-001'.

tool_activity_log.description: Array of AI tool activity records — one entry per tool active at the decision point. At least one entry required.

tool_id.description: Vendor-assigned unique identifier for the AI tool.

tool_version.description: Version of the model or tool at time of decision.

activation_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 datetime — when the tool became active in this encounter.

activation_trigger.description: Why the tool became active. Required. Distinguishes passive background tools from actively invoked tools.

output_surfaced.description: True if the tool produced output visible to the clinician at the decision point.

output_attended.description: True if the clinician attended to the output at the moment of decision. Null when not capturable.

output_type.description: Type of output produced by the tool.

output_summary_hash.description: SHA-256 of the canonical UTF-8 JSON representation of the tool output object.

training_data_version.description: Version of training data used by the model.

last_validation_date.description: ISO 8601 date of last model validation.

detected.description: True if vendor has flagged model drift since last validation.

detected_date.description: ISO 8601 date when drift was detected.

judgment_capture.description: Array of clinician judgment records — one entry per tool in tool_activity_log that surfaced output.

tool_id.description: Foreign key — must match a tool_id in tool_activity_log.

capture_method.description: How the judgment signal was captured.

inference_pattern.description: Required when capture_method is 'inferred'. Named inference pattern used.

capture_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the judgment signal was captured.

judgment.description: Clinician judgment on the AI output.

modification_flag.description: True if the clinician modified AI output before acting on it.

override_reason_code.description: Structured reason code when judgment is 'rejected'. Follow implementing organization controlled vocabulary.

override_reason_free_text.description: Optional free text reason. Must be de-identified before storage.

time_to_judgment_seconds.description: Elapsed seconds between output surfacing and judgment capture.

deferred_until.description: Required when judgment is 'deferred'. Expected resolution datetime.

prior_judgment_summary.description: Prior judgment history for this tool and patient pair. Enables alert fatigue detection.

provenance_chain.description: Documents the lineage of data that informed the AI output.

input_data_sources.description: Array of data sources that informed the AI output.

fhir_resource_types.description: Array of FHIR R4 resource types accessed e.g. ['MedicationRequest', 'AllergyIntolerance'].

prompt_captured.description: True if the prompt or query sent to the AI model was captured.

prompt_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the prompt if captured. Never store raw prompts — PHI risk.

context_window_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the full context provided to the model.

retrieval_sources.description: Array of knowledge base or retrieval sources used e.g. ['clinical-guidelines-db-v2026-Q1'].

hipaa_covered_entity.description: True if the emitting organization is a HIPAA covered entity.

baa_on_file.description: True if a Business Associate Agreement is on file at time of event generation.

retention_policy_years.description: Minimum retention period in years per applicable jurisdiction. US minimum 6, EU minimum 10, AU minimum 7.

data_residency.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code where the event is stored e.g. 'US'.

compliance_status.description: Current compliance status against this framework.

last_assessed.description: ISO 8601 date of last compliance assessment.

immutability_verified.description: True if the storage layer has confirmed append-only immutability. Set by storage layer, not emitting system.

encryption_at_rest.description: True if the event is encrypted at rest.

encryption_in_transit.description: True if the event is encrypted in transit.

review_record.description: Optional. Secondary human review record — supervisor sign-off, pharmacist co-sign, or compliance spot-check.

reviewer_id_hash.description: SHA-256 salted hash of reviewer identifier.

review_notes_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of reviewer notes if captured.

promotion_conditions.description: Optional. Records whether AI output crossed a threshold triggering conditional escalation. Required when activation_trigger is 'automatic' and confidence_score exceeds 0.8, or output_type is 'alert' or 'recommendation'.

threshold_type.description: The type of threshold evaluated e.g. 'confidence_score', 'risk_score'.

threshold_value.description: The threshold value that was evaluated.

threshold_met.description: True if the threshold was met or exceeded.

escalation_required.description: True if policy requires human escalation given the threshold result.

escalation_triggered.description: True if escalation was actually triggered.

escalation_target.description: Role or system receiving the escalation e.g. 'charge_nurse', 'pharmacy_review_queue'.

_extensions.description: Optional. Freeform object for implementer-specific fields. Must not override or conflict with core schema fields.

fields.description: Extension fields. Structure defined by implementer.
